audit_items.proof_trace: RPC-imported columns bypass observed_column_sidecars cache. Gossip path calls observe_gossip_data_column (L521 data_column_verification.rs) which updates the cache, but put_rpc_custody_columns only updates observed_slashable. A proposer can publish conflicting columns: column A via gossip gets observed, then column B (different data, same slot/index/proposer) via RPC passes all checks because observed_column_sidecars is never consulted on the RPC path.
audit_items.attack_scenario: Attacker proposes block B with data column C1 and publishes C1 via gossip (gets cached in observed_column_sidecars). Attacker then serves conflicting column C2 (same index, different data, same signature) via RPC to specific peers. RPC path imports C2 without checking observed_column_sidecars, allowing two contradictory columns for the same (slot, proposer, index) tuple to coexist across the network.
